title: DSDS Entity Metadata
description: The metadata object every entity type accepts — a plain object keyed by field name. Each field has its own schema file in this directory (status, since, last-updated, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, links, governance, doc-origin); `extends` points at the shared entityExtends definition. Every field is optional, and each can appear only once. Simple fields are just a value or array ('since': '1.0.0', 'tags': [...]); status, lastUpdated, and docOrigin take a short string for the common case or an object for more detail. An entity's `description` isn't metadata — it lives at the top level, next to `identifier` and `name`.

entityMetadata.description: Optional metadata for an entity. Include only the fields you need: lifecycle (status, since, lastUpdated), classification (category, tags, aliases), compact display (summary, thumbnail, preview), inheritance (extends), links (links), and who's accountable and how the docs were made (governance, docOrigin).
